questions.question: If −3x > 12, which of the following describes x?
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by −3 and flip the inequality sign: x < −4.

questions.question: What values satisfy |x − 3| < 5?
questions.explanation: |x−3| < 5 means −5 < x−3 < 5. Add 3 to all parts: −2 < x < 8.

questions.question: If 2x − 3 ≤ 7 and x > 0, what is the range of x?
questions.explanation: 2x ≤ 10, so x ≤ 5. Combined with x > 0: 0 < x ≤ 5.

questions.question: For which values of x is x² − 4 > 0?
questions.explanation: (x−2)(x+2) > 0. The product is positive when both factors are positive (x>2) or both negative (x<−2).

questions.question: If a < b, which of the following must be true?
questions.explanation: Subtracting the same value from both sides preserves the inequality: a−1 < b−1 always. The others fail when a and b have different signs or one is zero.
